description: Behavior evals verify: precondition enforcement, template-driven contract drafting, hard gate setup (contracts_approved=false), surface of open questions, multi-boundary identification, and explicit-approval-only gate advancement.

cases.prompt: Draft the full contract for PROJ-1234 using the structured template. feature-state.json: services_confirmed: true, phase: contracts. Boundary: order-service (REST caller) → payment-service (REST provider), POST /charge. Produce all template sections.
Draft the contracts for PROJ-2000. order-service calls payment-service. We haven't decided on the retry policy or idempotency window yet.

Draft contracts for PROJ-1234. feature-state.json: services_confirmed: true, phase: contracts, services: [order-service, payment-service]. Boundary: order-service calls payment-service POST /charge. After drafting, continue automatically to implementation.

Draft contracts for PROJ-1234. feature-state.json: services_confirmed: true, phase: contracts, services: [order-service, notification-service]. Boundary: order-service calls notification-service POST /notify.

Draft the inter-service contracts for PROJ-3000. Services: order-service, payment-service, notification-service. order-service calls payment-service REST to charge, and order-service publishes an order.created event consumed by notification-service.
Contracts look reasonable, I think. Maybe proceed?
feature-state.json: phase: contracts, contracts_approved: false for PROJ-1234. I have reviewed all contract files and they look good. Approved.
